Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and glycosuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes unlikely. Mild hyperchloremia is present. Recommend further nephrological evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and assessment for non-diabetic causes of renal dysfunction.
